name: Event-Driven Architecture
def: Components communicate by producing and consuming events rather than direct calls.
problem: Decouple producers from consumers in time and space for scalability and extensibility.
mechanism: Events flow through a broker/log (Kafka, queues); consumers subscribe and react. Choreography (events) or orchestration (a coordinator) coordinates workflows.
limits: Eventual consistency; hard to trace flows
failures: Lost ordering; no idempotency on consumers
myths: "Events make systems simpler" — they trade coupling for distributed-tracing complexity
